heading: Law enforcement training programs
label: 10159 Law enforcement training programs

children.text: that is provided by the Attorney General under section 10381(n)(3) of this title; or1

children.label: A
children.text: provided by a public or private entity, including the personnel of a law enforcement agency or law enforcement training academy of a State or unit of local government who have been trained to offer training programs or courses under section 10381(n)(3) of this title; and1

children.label: B
children.text: certified by the Attorney General under section 10381(n)(2) of this title.1

children.label: i
children.text: costs associated with conducting a certified training program or course or, subject to paragraph (2), a certified training program or course that provides continuing education; and

children.label: ii
children.text: attendance by law enforcement officers or covered mental health professionals at a certified training program or course, including a course provided by a law enforcement training academy of a State or unit of local government;

children.label: B
children.text: procure a certified training program or course or, subject to paragraph (2), a certified training program or course that provides continuing education on 1 or more of the topics described in section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title;1

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a law enforcement agency of a unit of local government that employs fewer than 50 employees (determined on a full-time equivalent basis), pay for the costs of overtime accrued as a result of the attendance of a law enforcement officer or covered mental health professional at a certified training program or course for which the costs associated with conducting the certified training program or course are paid using amounts provided under this section;

children.label: D
children.text: pay for the costs of developing mechanisms to comply with the reporting requirements established under subsection (d), in an amount not to exceed 5 percent of the total amount of the grant award; and

children.label: E
children.text: pay for the costs associated with participation in the voluntary National Use-of-Force Data Collection of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, in an amount not to exceed 5 percent of the total amount of the grant award, if a law enforcement agency of the State or unit of local government is not already reporting to the National Use-of-Force Data Collection.

children.heading: Requirements for use for continuing education
children.label: 2 Requirements for use for continuing education

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “covered topic” means a topic covered under the curricula developed or identified under clause (i), (ii), or (iv) of section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title.1

children.heading: Requirement to provide initial training
children.label: B Requirement to provide initial training
children.text: A State or unit of local government shall ensure that all officers who have been employed with the State or unit of local government for at least 2 years have received training as part of a certified training program or course on all covered topics before the State or unit of local government uses amounts received under a grant under paragraph (1) for continuing education with respect to any covered topic.

children.heading: Start date of availability of funding
children.label: C Start date of availability of funding

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), a State or unit of local government may not use amounts received under a grant under paragraph (1) for continuing education with respect to a covered topic until the date that is 2 years after .December 27, 2022

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: A State or unit of local government may use amounts received under a grant under paragraph (1) for continuing education with respect to a covered topic during the 2-year period beginning on , if the State or unit of local government has complied with subparagraph (B) using amounts available to the State or unit of local government other than amounts received under a grant under paragraph (1).December 27, 2022

children.heading: Maintaining relationships with local mental health organizations
children.label: 3 Maintaining relationships with local mental health organizations
children.text: A State or unit of local government that receives funds under this section shall establish and maintain relationships between law enforcement officers and local mental health organizations and health care services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Of the total amount appropriated to carry out this section for a fiscal year, the Attorney General shall allocate funds to each State in proportion to the total number of law enforcement officers in the State that are employed by the State or a unit of local government within the State, as compared to the total number of law enforcement officers in the United States.

children.heading: Retention of funds for training for State law enforcement officers proportional to number of State officers
children.label: 2 Retention of funds for training for State law enforcement officers proportional to number of State officers
A
children.text: the total number of law enforcement officers employed by the State; to

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of law enforcement officers in the State that are employed by the State or a unit of local government within the State.

children.heading: Provision of funds for training for local law enforcement officers
children.label: 3 Provision of funds for training for local law enforcement officers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State shall make available to units of local government in the State for the purposes described in this section the amounts remaining after a State retains funds under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: to facilitate offering a certified training program or course or, subject to subsection (b)(2), a certified training program or course that provide continuing education in 1 or more of the topics described in section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title to law enforcement officers employed by the unit of local government; or2So in original. Probably should be “provides”.21

children.label: ii
children.text: for the costs of training local law enforcement officers, including through law enforcement training academies of States and units of local government, to conduct a certified training program or course.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: C Consultation
children.text: The Attorney General, in consultation with relevant law enforcement agencies of States and units of local government, associations that represent individuals with mental or behavioral health diagnoses or individuals with disabilities, labor organizations, professional law enforcement organizations, local law enforcement labor and representative organizations, law enforcement trade associations, mental health and suicide prevention organizations, family advocacy organizations, and civil rights and civil liberties groups, shall develop criteria governing the allocation of funds to units of local government under this paragraph, which shall ensure that the funds are distributed as widely as practicable in terms of geographical location and to both large and small law enforcement agencies of units of local government.

children.heading: Announcement of allocations
children.label: D Announcement of allocations
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which a State receives an award under paragraph (1), the State shall announce the allocations of funds to units of local government under subparagraph (A). A State shall submit to the Attorney General a report explaining any delays in the announcement of allocations under this subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: shall include the number of law enforcement officers employed by the unit of local government that have completed a certified training program or course, including a certified training program or course provided on or before the date on which the Attorney General begins certifying training programs and courses under section 10381(n)(2) of this title, the topics covered in those courses, and the number of officers who received training in each topic;1

children.label: B
children.text: may, at the election of the unit of local government, include the number of law enforcement officers employed by the unit of local government that have completed a certified training program or course using funds provided from a source other than the grants described under subsection (b), the topics covered in those courses, and the number of officers who received training in each topic;

children.label: C
children.text: shall include the total number of law enforcement officers employed by the unit of local government;

children.label: D
children.text: shall include a description of any barriers to providing training on the topics described in section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title;1

children.label: i
children.text: pre-training and post-training tests that assess relevant knowledge and skills covered in the training curricula, as specified in section 10381(n)(1) of this title; and1

children.label: ii
children.text: follow-up evaluative assessments to determine the degree to which participants in the training apply, in their jobs, the knowledge and skills gained in the training; and

children.label: F
children.text: shall include the amount of funds received by the unit of local government under subsection (c)(3) and a tentative plan for training all law enforcement officers employed by the unit of local government using available and anticipated funds.

children.label: A
children.text: any report the State receives from a unit of local government under paragraph (1); and

children.label: i
children.text: indicating the number of law enforcement officers employed by the State that have completed a certified training program or course, including a certified training program or course provided on or before the date on which the Attorney General begins certifying training programs or courses under section 10381(n)(2) of this title, the topics covered in those courses, and the number of officers who received training in each topic, including, at the election of the State, a certified training program or course using funds provided from a source other than the grants described under subsection (b);1

children.label: ii
children.text: indicating the total number of law enforcement officers employed by the State;

children.label: I
children.text: pre-training and post-training tests that assess relevant knowledge and skills covered in the training curricula, as specified in section 10381(n)(1) of this title; and1

children.label: II
children.text: follow-up evaluative assessments to determine the degree to which participants in the training apply, in their jobs, the knowledge and skills gained in the training;

children.label: iv
children.text: discussing any barriers to providing training on the topics described in section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title; and1

children.label: v
children.text: indicating the amount of funding retained by the State under subsection (c)(2) and providing a tentative plan for training all law enforcement officers employed by the State using available and anticipated funds.

children.heading: Reporting tools
children.label: 3 Reporting tools
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Attorney General shall develop a portal through which the data required under paragraphs (1) and (2) may be collected and submitted.December 27, 2022

children.heading: Reports on the use of de-escalation tactics and other techniques
children.label: 4 Reports on the use of de-escalation tactics and other techniques
i
children.text: reporting requirements on interactions in which de-escalation tactics and other techniques in curricula developed or identified under section 10381(n)(1) of this title are used by each law enforcement agency that receives funding under this section; and1

children.label: ii
children.text: mechanisms for each law enforcement agency to submit such reports to the Department of Justice.

children.label: aa
children.text: the cost of collecting and reporting the information; and

children.label: AA
children.text: the objectives of the training have been met; and

children.label: BB
children.text: the training reduced or eliminated the risk of serious physical injury to officers, subjects, and third parties; and

children.label: II
children.text: the demographic and other relevant information about the officer and subjects involved in the interaction that shall be included in such a report; and

children.label: I
children.text: is in compliance with all applicable Federal and State confidentiality laws; and

children.label: II
children.text: does not disclose the identities of law enforcement officers, subjects, or third parties.

children.heading: Review of reporting requirements
children.label: C Review of reporting requirements
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and every 2 years thereafter, the Attorney General, in consultation with the entities specified under subparagraph (A), shall review and consider updates to the reporting requirements.December 27, 2022

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An entity receiving funds under this section that fails to file a report as required under paragraph (1) or (2), as applicable and as determined by the Attorney General, shall not be eligible to receive funds under this section for a period of 2 fiscal years.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall be construed to prohibit a State that fails to file a report as required under paragraph (2), and is not eligible to receive funds under this section, from making funding available to a unit of local government of the State under subsection (c)(3), if the unit of local government has complied with the reporting requirements.

children.heading: Implementation report
children.label: 1 Implementation report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and each year thereafter in which grants are made under this section, the Attorney General shall submit a report to Congress on the implementation of activities carried out under this section.December 27, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: the number, amounts, and recipients of awards the Attorney General has made or intends to make using funds authorized under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the selection criteria the Attorney General has used or intends to use to select recipients of awards using funds authorized under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of law enforcement officers of a State or unit of local government who were not able to receive training on the topics described in section 10381(n)(1)(A) of this title due to unavailability of funds and the amount of funds that would be required to complete the training; and1

children.label: D
children.text: the nature, frequency, and amount of information that the Attorney General has collected or intends to collect under subsection (d).

children.heading: Privacy protections
children.label: 3 Privacy protections
children.text: A report under paragraph (1) shall not disclose the identities of individual law enforcement officers who received, or did not receive, training under a certified training program or course.

children.heading: National Institute of Justice study
children.label: f National Institute of Justice study
A
children.text: a process evaluation of training implementation, which shall include an analysis of the share of officers who participated in the training, the degree to which the training was administered in accordance with the curriculum, and the fidelity with which the training was applied in the field; and

children.label: B
children.text: an impact evaluation of the training, which shall include an analysis of the impact of the training on interactions between law enforcement officers and the public, any factors that prevent or preclude law enforcement officers from successfully de-escalating law enforcement interactions, and any recommendations on modifications to the training curricula and methods that could improve outcomes.

children.heading: National Institute of Justice access to portal
children.label: 2 National Institute of Justice access to portal
children.text: For the purposes of preparing the report under paragraph (1), the National Institute of Justice shall have direct access to the portal developed under subsection (d)(3).

children.heading: Privacy protections
children.label: 3 Privacy protections
children.text: The study under paragraph (1) shall not disclose the identities of individual law enforcement officers who received, or did not receive, training under a certified training program or course.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: Not more than 1 percent of the amount appropriated to carry out this section during any fiscal year shall be made available to conduct the study under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: the process for developing and identifying curricula under section 10381(n)(1) of this title, including the effectiveness of the consultation by the Attorney General with the agencies, associations, and organizations identified under section 10381(n)(1)(C) of this title;11

children.label: B
children.text: the certification of training programs and courses under section 10381(n)(2) of this title, including the development of the process for certification and its implementation;1

children.label: C
children.text: the training of law enforcement personnel under section 10381(n)(3) of this title, including the geographic distribution of the agencies that employ the personnel receiving the training and the sizes of those agencies;1

children.label: D
children.text: the allocation of funds under subsection (c), including the geographic distribution of the agencies that receive funds and the degree to which both large and small agencies receive funds; and

children.label: E
children.text: the amount of funding distributed to agencies compared with the amount appropriated under this section, the amount spent for training, and whether plans have been put in place by the recipient agencies to use unspent available funds.

children.heading: GAO access to portal
children.label: 2 GAO access to portal
children.text: For the purposes of preparing the report under paragraph (1), the Comptroller General of the United States shall have direct access to the portal developed under subsection (d)(3).
$40,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and
$50,000,000 for fiscal year 2026.
